mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 545e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B58B30C engine typically costs £4,000–£6,500 supply only, with used units available from £3,000–£5,000. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on the workshop. Note that the hybrid battery is a separate component — replacement costs £3,000–£8,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The 545e's B58 is the same engine used in the 540i and Toyota Supra. The main concerns are the cooling system (water pump, hoses) and battery degradation over time. With good maintenance, the engine itself is exceptionally durable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 545e?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system leaks and water pump failure, high-voltage battery degradation (reduced electric range after 5–8 years), and oil filter housing leaks. The B58 is fundamentally robust — these are maintenance items, not catastrophic design flaws.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 545e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 545e values remain strong — a well-kept G30 LCI is often worth £20,000–£35,000+. A £4,000–£6,500 reconditioned engine is only 15–25% of the vehicle's value — well within the 70% BMW threshold. However, if the hybrid battery also needs replacing, total costs can approach 40–50% of the car's value. Evaluate both components before deciding.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 545e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is B58B30C — it's the only engine used in the G30 LCI 545e (2020–2023). You can find it on the VIN plate, in the vehicle documentation, or on a label on the engine itself. Production ended in 2023.

mainEntity.name: Can I replace just the hybrid battery, or does it need the engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The hybrid battery is a separate component from the engine — it can be replaced independently. Battery replacement costs £3,000–£8,000 depending on whether you need cell module replacement or a full battery pack. BMW's battery warranty typically covers 8 years / 100,000 miles in the UK — check if your car is still covered.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current 545e market. Note that these figures are for the combustion engine only — the hybrid battery is a separate component (see FAQ). Used B58 engines from the 545e are relatively scarce due to the model's limited production run.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 545e engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The hybrid system adds complexity — ensure your chosen specialist has PHEV experience.

problems.title: Cooling System Leaks (Water Pump and Hoses)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, rising temperature gauge, warning light, steam from the engine bay, heater not working properly
problems.recommendation: The B58's cooling system is its most common weak point. The water pump can fail, and the plastic coolant hoses can become brittle. The 545e's hybrid system adds extra cooling complexity, making regular inspection essential. Replace the water pump preventatively at around 60,000–80,000 miles. Check coolant levels regularly and investigate any drop immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: High-Voltage Battery Degradation
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric range, longer charging times, warning lights, reduced power in EV mode, battery management system errors
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (battery replacement is separate from engine replacement)
problems.recommendation: Like all PHEVs, the 545e's 11.6 kWh battery will degrade over time. Battery capacity loss typically becomes noticeable after 5–7 years or 100,000 km. Symptoms include reduced electric range and longer charging times. BMW's battery management system is sophisticated, but regular use and avoiding frequent 100% charges can extend battery life. If you notice significant range loss, have the battery health checked by a BMW specialist. Note that battery replacement is separate from engine replacement — and can be costly.

failureLink.label: Read the full hybrid battery failure guide

problems.title: Oil Filter Housing Leaks
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, low oil pressure warning, coolant loss, engine warning light
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (oil filter housing replacement, including intake manifold removal)
problems.recommendation: The B58's oil filter housing is a known weak point. The housing can crack, causing coolant or oil leaks. Because it sits under the intake manifold, replacement is labour-intensive. Check for oil spots regularly and investigate any drop in fluid levels promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil filter housing failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 545e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with correct BMW-spec oil — the B58 is reliable but unforgiving of neglect
* Evidence of cooling system maintenance — water pump or hoses replaced
* Battery health report — ask the seller for a battery capacity test
* A well-maintained example where the previous owner understood the PHEV's unique requirements
avoidIf: * Evidence of repeated coolant top-ups without a diagnosed leak — the cooling system is the B58's Achilles' heel
* Reduced electric range compared to factory figures — battery degradation may be advanced
* Poor service history — these engines are sensitive to oil quality
* Any engine warning light without a clear diagnosis
* No evidence of battery health checks — PHEV batteries are expensive to replace

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 545e is a genuinely remarkable car — combining the B58's legendary smoothness and tunability with electric efficiency. The engine itself is one of BMW's most reliable, but the hybrid system adds complexity. Battery degradation is the main long-term concern — budget for it at around 8–10 years. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and the 545e will reward you with performance and efficiency that few cars can match.

cta.label: → Compare 545e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 545e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (cooling system warning)
2. Service History — including evidence of cooling system maintenance and oil changes
3. Battery Health — Ask for a battery capacity test or health report
4. Electric Range — Does it match the expected 30+ miles? (range loss indicates battery degradation)
5. Oil Spots — Any oil under the car? (oil filter housing or valve cover leak warning)
6. Cold Start — Any rattles? (timing chain issues are rare on B58 but possible)
7. Charging Behaviour — Does it charge normally? Any charging errors?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
